title: Architecture Decision Record
description: Schema for structured Architecture Decision Records (ADRs). ADRs document significant architectural decisions, their context, rationale, and consequences. Designed for the repo's JSON-first documentation policy — human-readable renderings are generated from these canonical JSON sources.

schema_version.description: Version of the ADR schema used at creation time

title.description: Short descriptive title of the decision in sentence case

status.description: Lifecycle status of the decision. proposed: under review. accepted: active and governing. deprecated: no longer recommended but not yet replaced. superseded: replaced by a later ADR.

date.description: Date the ADR was originally proposed (ISO 8601 date, YYYY-MM-DD)

context.description: The context and problem statement that motivated this decision. What forces are at play? What constraints exist? What options were considered?

decision.description: The decision that was made and its full rationale. What is the change? Why was this option chosen over the alternatives?

consequences.description: The consequences of this decision — what becomes easier, harder, or different. Include both positive and negative consequences.

supersedes.description: ADR IDs that this decision supersedes (e.g. ['0003']). Empty array if none.

superseded_by.description: ADR IDs that supersede this decision. Empty array if still active.

authors.description: Authors who contributed to this decision

examples.title: Shared build convention layer with tsconfig base and vite preset
examples.context: The monorepo has ~25 packages with independent tsconfig files and 6 vite-based packages with duplicated plugin configuration. Build tool diversity is architecturally necessary (each tool serves a distinct output target), but the absence of shared configuration creates onboarding friction and makes cross-package build changes risky.
examples.decision: Create a root tsconfig.base.json with shared compiler settings and a shared vite preset package for the 6 SolidJS-based web apps. Keep all existing build tools — they are idiomatic for their targets. Per-package configs extend the shared base and override only what is unique (entry point, output name, environment-specific settings).
examples.consequences: Positive: reduces configuration duplication, makes compiler option changes consistent across the monorepo, simplifies onboarding. Negative: adds an indirection layer that may slow down local debugging of compiler issues, requires all package tsconfig updates to consider the shared base.
